mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (logistic regression coefficient insignificant at p<0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly payroll employment growth sustains household income and consumption spending, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day direction (Rank IC < 0.02) across all macro cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3% are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent upward trends in quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales confirm broad-based demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative or flat 20-day returns in > 60% of aligned observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply chain contraction and corporate earnings pressure, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops below -0.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances.
